quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)
Never set running text below 12px.

relations.note: Line-height minimums apply alongside the email type scale sizes.
Hierarchy distinction relies on the defined type scale levels.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
*Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font)
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos
Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.

rules.intent: Preserve the approved cross-medium font hierarchy.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headers use Agenda (headline.font = Agenda) in print and Nunito Sans (body.font = Nunito Sans) with bolder weights, Title Case, in digital/email; Agenda may only be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline (campaign_headline.font = Agenda). Body copy and footers/fine print use Nunito Sans across print and digital. Word/PPT/Excel fallback is Aptos.

quotes: Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
**[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it

rules.intent: Ensure consistent typeface with graceful fallback across email clients.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email must use the CSS font stack font.email.stack = 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
use sparingly, never for primary content
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Enforce the approved email type scale and minimum readable sizes.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email type scale: H1 (section hero headline) 28px/36px (~130%) Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold; H2 (section heading) 22px/30px (~136%) Nunito Sans Bold; H3 (subhead) 18px/26px (~144%) Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold; Body 16px/24px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular — 16px (size.body.min = 16px) is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better"); secondary/supporting body 14px/21px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular used sparingly, never for primary content; footnote/reference text 12px/18px (150%) must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated from it. Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule)
**For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px)

rules.intent: Guarantee accessible readability of body copy.
rules.rule_text: Line height must be a minimum of 150% for all body copy (body.line_height min = 150%), a brand accessibility rule. For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further — [GENERAL] use 160% (line_height.light_on_dark = 160%, e.g. 16px/26px).

applies_when.value: two adjacent text blocks at same size+weight

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Maintain legible reading order via distinct hierarchy levels.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
